title: Joby Stock Earnings: Joby Aviation Beats EPS for Q1 2024
text: Joby Aviation beat earnings per share expectations for Q1 2024, reporting EPS of -14 cents versus the estimate of -17 cents. The company reported revenue of $25,000, with a net loss of $95 million for the quarter. At the end of the first quarter of 2024, Joby maintained a strong balance sheet with $924 million in cash and short-term investments. The company's second production prototype aircraft rolled off the Pilot Production Line and was expected to join the first production prototype at Edwards Air Force Base later in the year. Two further aircraft were in final assembly. Joby became the first electric air taxi company to have its final airworthiness criteria published by the FAA. The company completed over 1,500 full-scale eVTOL flights under its pre-production flight test program. The company's strong balance sheet and regulatory progress provided confidence in its ability to reach commercial operations. Manufacturing expansion was underway at facilities in California and Ohio. Strategic partnerships with Uber, Delta, and Toyota were supporting the company's commercialization roadmap.
